title: Applied Pine Board Fireplace Mantel
summary: A standalone shelf mantel built entirely from stock pine boards and applied moulding — a small applied piece, not the floor-to-ceiling framed surround built elsewhere in this catalog.
description:
This mantel is a self-contained applied shelf, not a framed structure built around an actual firebox — it's sized and built to mount to a wall on its own, giving a room a mantel shelf even where no fireplace exists. Lay out the top board and legs first, joining them on the back side with pocket holes if you have a jig, or glue and finish nails if not.

Stacking several 1x3 front decorative pieces is what gives the face its layered, built-up look rather than a single flat board — **glue these on rather than nailing**, since nails are likely to split this thin trim stock. A 1x8 board and short 1x3 blocking pieces added to the back bring the mantel out to its full depth, and this is also where you'll set the reveal: lay the crown moulding against the front assembly to check where the 1x4 trim piece lands, aiming for about a 2-1/2" reveal below the crown once it's mitered on.

Crown moulding wraps the top with mitered outside corners, and an optional inset square of edge moulding on the front face — glued in place — adds a final decorative detail if you want it.

tools.note: Optional; glue and finish nails work without one.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Lay out the top and legs
steps.body: Attach the legs to the top board on the back side, using pocket holes if you have a jig, or glue and finish nails if not.

steps.title: Add depth at the back
steps.body: Attach the back depth board and back blocking pieces to bring the mantel out to full depth.

steps.title: Set the front trim reveal
steps.body: Lay the crown moulding against the front assembly to check placement, then attach the front trim pieces to reveal about 2-1/2" below where the crown will sit.

steps.title: Glue on the decorative front pieces
steps.body: Glue the front decorative pieces in place rather than nailing them, since nails can split this thin stock.

steps.title: Attach the side trim
steps.body: Nail the side trim boards on, keeping outside edges flush.

steps.title: Add the crown moulding
steps.body: Cut the crown moulding to fit and attach around the top, mitering the outside corners.

steps.title: Add the inset moulding (optional)
steps.body: Cut the edge moulding to fit the inset square on the front face and glue in place.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Applied pine board fireplace mantel shelf
